prompt: Create a vertical fine-art editorial portrait titled The Lady of Red Poppies: a pale-skinned woman standing in a misty red poppy field, framed from chest to head in a close-up composition. Her face is deliberately obscured by a centered opaque square color block, a muted reddish-brown rectangle covering from forehead to chin, creating a surreal censored-art effect. She wears an ornate high-neck white Victorian-inspired lace blouse with raised floral embroidery, puffed shoulders, long lace cuffs, and three small brown buttons down the front. Both slender hands are raised to her head, fingertips near her temples, holding a crown-like cluster of vivid scarlet red poppies around her hair; count 6 prominent poppy blossoms visible around the top and sides of the head, with translucent crinkled petals and dark black centers. Her hair is mostly hidden but appears very light platinum blonde or white, glowing softly behind the flowers. The foreground contains 4 out-of-focus red poppies at the bottom and right edges, while the background is a shallow-depth-of-field field of blurred red poppies fading into cool gray-blue mist and vertical tree-like streaks. Use cinematic natural light, soft overcast glow, dreamy bokeh, realistic skin and fabric texture, high detail in lace and petals, strong contrast between red flowers and white clothing, melancholic romantic mood, centered symmetrical framing, 4:5 aspect ratio, photorealistic with painterly fine-art color grading.
